mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes is negative over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not exceed 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer prices indicates strong demand outpacing supply, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side contraction that rapidly depresses equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and consumer spending power, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions correlate with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 5.0% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in real GDP and housing starts indicate broad-based economic strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates alongside a stable unemployment rate indicate a soft landing strategy, which preserves equity valuations and generates positive forward returns.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, this signal predicts negative returns in over 55% of cases, or the hit rate drops below 50%.
